briefing.headline: Rig up a Carolina Rig — finesse winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Carolina Rig is a strong choice for winter with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag devoid of deadstick pauses.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag d
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on riprap, creek channel, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Fishes Carolina rig in 4 to 25 feet of water, but most of the time works it in 8 to 12 feet; holds rod to his side at three o'clock position during entire retrieve.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on riprap, creek channel, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: On lowland reservoirs with grass (Guntersville, Toledo Bend, Okeechobee), bass don't leave shallow areas even in extreme cold. They stage in slightly deeper areas adjacent to spawning flats — drains, ditches, depressions, holes in the grass. ...

Wheeler: On highland reservoirs like Cherokee, smallmouth live around holes/depressions (20 ft around the side) — if rock is around them the smallies live there. If a rock vein goes into the middle, they live on those veins. The holes harbor the b...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal winter is the toughest bite of the year. Fish retreat to the deepest structure available (20-35ft+) and barely move. Target steep structure where fish can access deep water without traveling — bluffs, vertical channel walls, bridge pilings.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
